# ReadingBat Core

> A Kotlin-based framework for creating interactive programming challenges, powering the ReadingBat platform for teaching Java, Kotlin, and Python programming concepts.

ReadingBat Core is a web application built with Ktor that serves programming challenges defined via a Kotlin DSL. Challenges are evaluated at runtime using JSR-223 script engines for Java, Kotlin, and Python. The platform supports user accounts, teacher-managed classes, progress tracking, and real-time WebSocket updates.

## Key Technologies

- Kotlin 2.3.10, Ktor 3.4.2 (CIO engine), Exposed ORM 1.1.1, PostgreSQL
- Kotlinx.html for server-side HTML generation (no templates)
- Kotest for testing, Playwright for E2E tests
- Gradle multi-module build with version catalog (libs.versions.toml)
- Java 17 toolchain

## Project Structure

Two Gradle submodules:

- **readingbat-core/**: Main application - Ktor web server, DSL engine, database layer, HTML page generation
- **readingbat-kotest/**: Test utilities module providing TestSupport helpers for Kotest-based integration tests

### Source Layout (readingbat-core)

- `dsl/` - Content DSL and challenge type definitions
- `pages/` - Server-side HTML page generation using Kotlinx.html
- `pages/js/` - Generated JavaScript for client-side interactivity
- `server/` - Core server infrastructure, routing, WebSocket handlers
- `server/ws/` - WebSocket routes for real-time updates
- `posts/` - Form handling
- `common/` - Shared utilities and value types

## Content DSL

The core abstraction is a Kotlin DSL (`readingBatContent { }`) that defines programming challenges:

- `ReadingBatContent` holds three `LanguageGroup`s: java, python, kotlin
- Each `LanguageGroup` contains `ChallengeGroup`s, each containing `Challenge`s
- Content can be loaded from local files (`FileSystemSource`) or GitHub repos (`GitHubContent`)
- `ContentDsl.kt` handles reading and evaluating DSL code via Kotlin scripting

## Configuration

Dual configuration system:

- `Property` (sealed class): HOCON-based properties from Ktor's ApplicationConfig
- `EnvVar` (enum): Environment variables that override HOCON values
- Secrets loaded from `secrets/secrets.env` (not committed)

## Database

Exposed ORM with PostgreSQL. Key tables: UsersTable, BrowserSessionsTable, UserSessionsTable, UserChallengeInfoTable, SessionChallengeInfoTable, UserAnswerHistoryTable, SessionAnswerHistoryTable, ClassesTable, EnrolleesTable, ServerRequestsTable, GeoInfosTable.

Migrations managed by Flyway in `src/main/resources/db/migration/`.

## Routing

Type-safe routing via Ktor `@Resource` annotations in `Locations.kt`: Language -> Language.Group -> Language.Group.Challenge (nested resources mapping to URL paths like `/content/java/Warmup-1/hello`).

## Authentication

Form-based auth with session management and OAuth (GitHub, Google). OAuth providers are auto-configured when credentials are present (not gated on production mode). Provider identification uses the `OAuthProvider` enum for type safety.

## Value Types

Extensive use of Kotlin `@JvmInline value class` for type safety: LanguageName, GroupName, ChallengeName, ChallengeMd5, Password, FullName, ResetId.

## Development

- Build: `make build` or `./gradlew build -xtest`
- Test: `make tests` or `./gradlew check`
- Run: `make run` or `./gradlew run`
- Lint: `make lint` (ktlint via Kotlinter)
- Format: `./gradlew formatKotlinMain formatKotlinTest`
- DB reset: `make dbreset`
- DB migrate: `make dbmigrate`

## Links

- [Source](https://github.com/readingbat/readingbat-core)
- [Template Repository](https://github.com/readingbat/readingbat-template)
